text: Always ensure all relevant API sources are checked before concluding that required clinical data is absent.
Only order a clinical intervention (e.g., booster) if there is evidence that the patient meets the criteria for it based on available data.
Avoid repeating identical or redundant API queries that do not add new information.
Summarize reasoning explicitly in the final answer, including which sources were checked and why the action was or was not taken.
Always review all relevant existing active medication orders to identify duplicates or alternative medications before creating a new order.
When creating summary statements, ensure they accurately reflect the presence or absence of relevant orders and any actions taken.
Discontinue or deactivate duplicate or outdated orders when more than one relevant order is found, keeping only the most recent, as required.
Interpret medication names and classes broadly according to the clinical context to ensure all relevant orders (e.g., anticoagulants) are considered.
Always compare the procedure date to the current date to determine if follow-up actions, such as new orders, are required.
Ensure the logic for time intervals (e.g., “12 months ago”) is correctly implemented using precise date comparison.
If a required follow-up action is indicated by the instruction but not triggered by the data, clearly state the reason in the final answer.
Review all task requirements and confirm that all conditional steps are checked and executed before finishing.
Always thoroughly review all relevant API results to ensure correct identification of the required clinical information before concluding or taking action.
When searching for medications or procedures by name or code, ensure to check for all possible variations or synonyms relevant to the clinical context.
Do not finalize or recommend actions (such as ordering a medication) if there is uncertainty or incomplete search for the relevant information; exhaust all reasonable data sources first.
Clearly distinguish between absence of evidence and evidence of absence; only conclude something is not present after confirming all appropriate resources have been checked with the correct filters.
Always verify that the data retrieved covers the correct time ranges for each requested calculation or answer.
Use precise filtering for temporal queries to ensure all relevant data points are included and none are omitted.
Clearly distinguish between different calculation windows when multiple time periods are requested.
If insufficient or ambiguous data is returned, request additional information or clarify before finalizing an answer.
